title: ferrotorch-core: gelu(default sigmoid approximation) f32 numerical accuracy gap vs torch.nn.GELU — surfaced by phase 2.5 (#767)
description: Phase 2.5 conformance probe shows that ferrotorch's gelu() defaults to GeluApproximate::Sigmoid (x * sigmoid(1.702 * x)) whereas PyTorch's torch.nn.GELU defaults to approximate='none' (exact erf-based). The fast-sigmoid form has ~6e-3 deviation from PyTorch's default — this is a documented design divergence rather than a bug, but the magnitude makes 'gelu' parity with PyTorch surprising. To close: either change ferrotorch's gelu() default to GeluApproximate::None to match PyTorch, or document the divergence prominently and have the conformance suite test gelu_with explicitly while not asserting on gelu()'s default.
